revision.text:
{{Infobox park | name = Musashiseki Park | photo = Musashizeki-park ike2006.JPG | photo_width = 250 | photo_caption = The park in 2006 | type =
| location = [[Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | publictransit = [[Higashi-Fushimi Station]] | coords = {{coord|35.727656|N|139.567809|E|type:landmark_region:JP||display=inline,title}} | area = {{convert|45,985|m2|acre}} | created = October 1938}}
{{nihongo|'''Musashiseki Park'''|武蔵関公園|Musashiseki Kōen}} is a public park in [[Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]].
==Overview== Fujimi Pond is located in the park, and like the ponds in [[Shakujii Park]], [[Inokashira Park]], and Zenpukuji Park, this pond is formed by spring water from the [[Musashino Terrace]]. It is possible to go boating on the pond. The park is also a famous spot for cherry blossoms viewing.
It opened as a private park in the [[Taisho era]], and since then, playground equipment and other facilities have been added.
==Access== * By train: 5 minutes’ walk from [[Higashi-Fushimi Station]] on the [[File:Seibu shinjuku logo.svg|18px|E]] [[Seibu Shinjuku Line]]
==See also== * [[Parks and gardens in Tokyo]] * [[List of national parks of Japan|National Parks of Japan]]
==References== {{Reflist}} * [https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/fukei/park/musashi/index.html Website of Nerima City (in Japanese)]
==External links== * [https://1000enpark.com/park/00236/ Website of Let’s Go to the Park with 1,000 Yen! (in Japanese)] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Tokyo]] {{Nerima}}

{{Short description|Park in Nerima, Tokyo, Japan}} {{Infobox park | name = Musashiseki Park | photo = 武蔵関公園「富士見池」（中之橋から北東ボート乗り場方面）練馬区.jpg | photo_width = 250 | photo_caption = The park in 2006 | type =
| location = [[Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]] | publictransit = [[Higashi-Fushimi Station]] | coords = {{coord|35.727656|N|139.567809|E|type:landmark_region:JP||display=inline,title}} | area = {{convert|45,985|m2|acre}} | created = October 1938}}
{{nihongo|'''Musashiseki Park'''|武蔵関公園|Musashiseki Kōen}} is a public park in [[Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]].
==Overview== Fujimi Pond is located in the park, and like the ponds in [[Shakujii Park]], [[Inokashira Park]], and Zenpukuji Park, this pond is formed by spring water from the [[Musashino Terrace]]. It is possible to go boating on the pond. The park is also a famous spot for cherry blossoms.
It opened as a private park in the [[Taishō era]], and since then, playground equipment and other facilities have been added.
==Access== * By train: 5 minutes’ walk from [[Higashi-Fushimi Station]] on the [[File:Seibu shinjuku logo.svg|18px|E]] [[Seibu Shinjuku Line]]
==See also== * [[Parks and gardens in Tokyo]] * [[List of national parks of Japan|National Parks of Japan]]
==References== {{Reflist}} * [https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/fukei/park/musashi/index.html Website of Nerima City (in Japanese)]
==External links== * [https://1000enpark.com/park/00236/ Website of Let’s Go to the Park with 1,000 Yen! (in Japanese)] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]] {{Nerima}} [[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Musashiseki Park]][[Category:qq]]

==Overview==
Fujimi Pond is located in the park, and like the ponds in [[:en:Shakujii Park|Shakujii Park]], [[:en:Inokashira Park|Inokashira Park]], and Zenpukuji Park, this pond is formed by spring water from the [[Musashino Terrace]]. It is possible to go boating on the pond. The park is also a famous spot for cherry blossoms.

It opened as a private park in the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]], and since then, playground equipment and other facilities have been added.

==Access==
* By train: 5 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Higashi-Fushimi Station|Higashi-Fushimi Station]] on the [[File:Seibu shinjuku logo.svg|18px|E]] [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]]

Fujimi Pond is located in the park, and like the ponds in {{ill|Shakujii Park|en|Shakujii Park|WD=Q4388784|lt=Shakujii Park|1=Shakujii Park}}, {{ill|Inokashira Park|en|Inokashira Park|WD=Q1200884|lt=Inokashira Park|1=Inokashira Park}}, and Zenpukuji Park, this pond is formed by spring water from the [[Musashino Terrace]]. It is possible to go boating on the pond. The park is also a famous spot for cherry blossoms.

It opened as a private park in the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}}, and since then, playground equipment and other facilities have been added.

==Access==
* By train: 5 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Higashi-Fushimi Station|en|Higashi-Fushimi Station|WD=Q5753520|lt=Higashi-Fushimi Station|1=Higashi-Fushimi Station}} on the [[File:Seibu shinjuku logo.svg|18px|E]] {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|WD=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line|1=Seibu Shinjuku Line}}

==See also==
* [[:en:Parks and gardens in Tokyo|Parks and gardens in Tokyo]]
* {{ill|List of national parks of Japan|en|List of national parks of Japan|WD=Q1071482|lt=National Parks of Japan|1=List of national parks of Japan}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}
* [https://www.city.nerima.tokyo.jp/kankomoyoshi/annai/fukei/park/musashi/index.html Website of Nerima City (in Japanese)]

==External links==
* [https://1000enpark.com/park/00236/ Website of Let’s Go to the Park with 1,000 Yen! (in Japanese)]

{{Nerima}}
